note: Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics (Nielsen Norman Group). Heuristic NAMES and the concept are public; article text is NOT reproduced. Cite by URL. Source: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/

heuristics.name: Visibility of system status
heuristics.summary: Keep users informed about what is going on through timely, appropriate feedback (saved/unsaved, loading, confirmed vs tentative).

heuristics.name: Match between system and the real world
heuristics.summary: Speak the users' language; follow real-world conventions and information ordering. For ortho: use 'doctor', 'banding', 'debond', not generic dental/provider jargon.

heuristics.name: User control and freedom
heuristics.summary: Provide clearly marked exits, undo, and redo; let users back out of actions without penalty (e.g. recover a half-finished reschedule).

heuristics.name: Consistency and standards
heuristics.summary: Follow platform and internal conventions; the same thing should behave the same way across locations/screens.

heuristics.name: Error prevention
heuristics.summary: Prevent problems before they occur — confirm destructive/double-booking actions, eliminate error-prone conditions (silent double-book, unsaved financial data).

heuristics.name: Recognition rather than recall
heuristics.summary: Minimize memory load; make options, icons, and color codes self-explanatory without hover or recall.

heuristics.name: Flexibility and efficiency of use
heuristics.summary: Accelerators (shortcuts, templates) for frequent tasks; let experts move fast while novices stay supported.

heuristics.name: Aesthetic and minimalist design
heuristics.summary: Don't clutter the interface with irrelevant info; every extra unit competes with the relevant. Default views should show only what this role/location needs.

heuristics.name: Help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from errors
heuristics.summary: Error messages in plain language, precise, constructive — no 'Error 400: Invalid Payload'.

heuristics.name: Help and documentation
heuristics.summary: Provide easy-to-search, task-focused help where needed — but the product should be usable without it.

heuristics.name: Form design best practices
heuristics.summary: Autosave / preserve input, inline validation, clear required fields, don't lose data on misclick — critical for live financial/insurance entry.

heuristics.name: Data tables & scheduling grids
heuristics.summary: Scannable rows/columns, clear status encoding, distinguish tentative vs confirmed, avoid ambiguous color-only signals.

heuristics.name: Response time limits
heuristics.summary: 0.1s = instant, 1s = keeps flow, 10s = limit of attention. Slow page loads during high-volume exam days break the live-conversation pace.
